_meta.applicable_law: UK GPSR 2024, UKCA, CPSR, CLP, FSA, MHRA, FCM Regulations
_meta.note: This file is a first-line keyword screen, not a substitute for professional compliance review. HIGH risk categories should never be auto-approved without seller verification.

requirements: 🧪 CPSR (Cosmetic Product Safety Report) required — EU/UK Cosmetic Regulation 1223/2009 retained
👤 Responsible Person (RP) declaration required under UK GPSR 2024
🏷️ Full INCI ingredient list required on packaging in descending order of weight
📋 Product Information File (PIF) must be maintained and accessible to UK authorities within 72 hours
⚗️ Lab safety testing required (challenge testing for water-based, stability testing for all)
🚫 No medical claims allowed (e.g. 'cures acne', 'treats eczema', 'heals scars')
📦 UK address of Responsible Person must appear on packaging — overseas-only address fails GPSR
🔬 Preservative challenge testing required for water-based or aqueous products
⚠️ Amazon Health & Personal Care may request CPSR + RP docs before listing approval
📄 CPNP notification (or UK equivalent SCPN) required before placing on UK market
🦷 UK Cosmetic Products Regulation: Maximum 0.1% hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) for OTC products
🚫 Products with >0.1% H2O2 are BANNED for direct consumer sale in UK — only registered dental professionals may supply
📋 CPSR required specifically for oral cosmetics with peroxide testing
⚠️ Amazon Health category requires prior approval — many sellers are gated out
🔬 Independent lab certificate confirming H2O2/carbamide peroxide concentration is mandatory
📄 SDS (Safety Data Sheet) required if any regulated chemicals present
🚫 Cannot make medical claims (e.g. 'removes plaque', 'prevents cavities')
👶 Must be labelled NOT FOR USE BY UNDER-18s if any peroxide present
📋 Food Business Operator (FBO) registration with local UK council required
🏷️ Full nutritional labelling required per UK Food Information Regulations 2014
🚫 No disease prevention/treatment claims (UK Advertising Standards Authority + EFSA)
⚠️ Novel Food authorisation may be required — check FSA Novel Food catalogue before listing
📄 SDS required if product contains controlled substances
🔬 Certificate of Analysis (CoA) per batch — Amazon may request
📦 Country of origin must be declared on label
⚠️ Amazon requires Responsible Person docs + CoA before listing in many supplement subcategories
🚫 CBD, novel proteins, novel extracts require explicit FSA authorisation
👶 Children's supplements require additional age-appropriate dosing warnings
🔌 UKCA marking required for UK market (replaced CE mark for GB after 31 Dec 2024)
📋 Declaration of Conformity (DoC) required and must accompany product on request
⚡ Mains-powered: must comply with BS 1363 (UK 3-pin plug) — NO EU 2-pin plugs accepted
🔋 Battery regulations: comply with UK Batteries Regulations 2008 — Producer Registration required
📄 WEEE compliance — register with an approved WEEE compliance scheme before placing on market
🔬 EMC testing certificate required (UK Electromagnetic Compatibility Regulations 2016)
🔥 Low Voltage Directive equivalent (UK Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 2016)
⚠️ Amazon UK Compliance team frequently requests UKCA/DoC docs — random audits common
🌍 RoHS UK compliance required (Restriction of Hazardous Substances)
📦 Responsible Person UK address required on product or packaging (GPSR 2024)
🔋 Lithium content/capacity must be declared on packaging (Wh and mAh)
✈️ Air transport: UN3480 (loose) or UN3481 (in equipment) — UN 38.3 test report required
📋 MSDS / Safety Data Sheet required
⚠️ Amazon enforces lithium battery documentation aggressively — listings without docs get suspended
🚫 Capacity limits: Amazon may restrict standalone batteries over certain Wh thresholds
📄 Battery Producer Registration (UK Batteries Regulations 2008) required
🔥 Cannot be sold as 'fire-resistant' or 'explosion-proof' without certified evidence
👶 If accessible to children: small parts warning + battery cover compliance
🏥 Anything marketed as treating/diagnosing/preventing disease requires MHRA registration
🇬🇧 UKCA mark with Notified Body number for Class IIa+ devices (UK MDR 2002 as amended)
📋 Clinical evaluation file + Technical documentation required
🚫 Cannot make medical claims without MHRA approval — even 'helps with', 'soothes', 'relieves' is risky
📄 Instructions For Use (IFU) in English required
⚠️ Amazon Medical category requires Brand Registry + pre-approval for most sellers
🏷️ Single-use, sterile, expiry, lot number must be on packaging where applicable
👤 UK Responsible Person required (UK MDR Part IVA)
🚫 Diagnostic in-vitro devices require additional IVDR compliance
📋 CBD ingestibles require FSA Novel Food authorisation — only authorised products may be sold
🚫 THC content must be below 1mg per closed container (UK Misuse of Drugs Regulations)
🏥 Cannot make medical claims — MHRA treats medicinal CBD as a licensed medicine
📄 Certificate of Analysis (CoA) per batch confirming THC + CBD levels mandatory
🌿 Source must be from EU-approved industrial hemp varieties
🚫 Amazon currently prohibits most ingestible CBD listings — read latest Amazon policy before listing
👶 Cannot be marketed to under-18s
🏷️ Daily intake limit recommendation must follow FSA guidance (currently 10mg/day)
🍳 Food Contact Materials (FCM) compliance required — Retained Regulation (EC) No 1935/2004
🚫 No unsubstantiated 'food safe', 'BPA free', 'PFOA free' claims without test evidence
📋 Declaration of Compliance (DoC) for food contact materials must be retained and provided on request
🔬 Migration testing required for non-stick coatings under Plastic Materials Regulation
🏷️ Material composition must be accurately described — silicone, melamine, ceramic, etc.
⚠️ Non-stick coatings: PTFE/PFOA claims scrutinised by Amazon and consumer protection bodies
📦 'Knife and fork' or 'glass' symbol indicates food contact — must use only where compliant
🚫 Melamine cookware: not for microwave/oven use — must be clearly labelled
🧸 UKCA marking MANDATORY — UK Toys (Safety) Regulations 2011
🔬 EN 71 safety testing required (Parts 1, 2, 3 minimum; Part 9 for organic chemicals)
⚠️ Age grading must be accurate, evidence-based, and marked on packaging
📋 Technical file with test reports must be retained for 10 years after last unit sold
🚫 Small parts warning (choking hazard) required if any part fits the small-parts cylinder under EN 71-1
📄 Declaration of Conformity required and traceable to manufacturer
🌍 RoHS UK + REACH compliance for chemicals (e.g. phthalates limits for under-3s items)
⚠️ Amazon Toys category requires prior approval for most sellers — Brand Registry helps
🔥 Stuffed toys: flammability standard BS 5852 if labelled for under-3s
👶 Sleep-related (cot mattress, bedding): BS EN 16890 for cots, BS 1877-10 for mattresses
🔥 Children's nightwear must meet BS 5722 flammability standard (Nightwear (Safety) Regulations 1985)
🏷️ Mandatory permanent flammability label: 'KEEP AWAY FROM FIRE' or compliant equivalent
🚫 Cannot make 'flame retardant' claims without certified evidence
📋 Test certificate from accredited UK lab required and retained for inspection
⚠️ Trading Standards regularly enforces — non-compliance leads to immediate Amazon takedown
🔞 UK Criminal Justice Act 1988 + Offensive Weapons Act 2019: sale to under-18s is a criminal offence
🚫 Cannot ship to UK addresses without age-verification at delivery (Amazon enforces via Restricted Products policy)
🚫 Zombie knives, flick knives, gravity knives, push daggers BANNED from sale (Offensive Weapons Act 2019)
🚫 Stealth knives (non-metallic) BANNED
⚠️ Sword sales restricted: curved blades over 50cm prohibited except for genuine antiques or hand-forged
📦 Packaging must include age-verification labelling
⚠️ Amazon requires Knife Restricted Product approval before listing
⚙️ UKCA marking required for powered/electrical tools (Supply of Machinery (Safety) Regs 2008)
📋 Declaration of Conformity required for powered tools
📄 Instructions for safe use must be included in English
🔬 EN safety standards compliance for professional/trade tools (e.g. EN 60745 for hand tools)
🚫 No misleading torque/load capacity claims without certified evidence
🚫 No weight loss or body composition claims without clinical evidence (ASA / CAP Code)
📋 UKCA marking required for protective equipment classified as PPE
🏋️ Personal Protective Equipment Regulations apply if PPE — Cat I/II/III conformity
⚠️ Avoid performance claims that cannot be substantiated
🪖 Helmets, shin guards: must meet relevant EN standard (e.g. EN 1078 for cycling helmets)
🐾 Pet food: must comply with UK Animal Feed Regulations + display registration number
🚫 No medicinal claims (e.g. 'cures fleas', 'treats anxiety') without VMD authorisation
📋 Pet food: ingredients in descending order + nutritional analysis required
📄 CITES check required for products containing animal-derived materials (ivory, certain leathers, exotic feathers)
🚫 Dog collars: cannot be electric/shock collars (banned in England from 2024)
⚠️ Pet medicines/veterinary products require VMD (Veterinary Medicines Directorate) authorisation
🚯 Aerosol Dispensers Regulations 2009 compliance required
🏷️ CLP (Classification, Labelling, Packaging) hazard pictograms + statements required
🚫 Cannot ship by air without dangerous goods classification (UN1950)
⚠️ Amazon restricts hazmat products — sellers need hazmat-approved Amazon account
🔥 Flammable contents: clear hazard warning required
📦 Packaging must withstand internal pressure of contents
🕶️ UKCA marking required as PPE (Category I for general sun protection)
🔬 EN ISO 12312-1 compliance for sunglasses
🏷️ UV protection rating must be accurate (UV400 = blocks UVA + UVB to 400nm)
🚫 Cannot claim 'medical' or 'prescription' without optometrist supply chain compliance
📋 Optical category (0-4) must be declared
👶 Children's eyewear: additional age-appropriate sizing + impact resistance requirements
🏷️ Food Information Regulations 2014: full allergen declaration (14 named allergens highlighted)
📅 'Best Before' or 'Use By' date required
📋 Nutritional information panel mandatory (per 100g/100ml)
🌍 Country of origin or place of provenance required
📋 FBO (Food Business Operator) registration with local council required
🚫 Health claims must be on approved EU/UK register (most are not)
🚫 No medical/disease claims
⚠️ Novel Foods authorisation required for ingredients not consumed significantly in EU before May 1997
📄 HACCP plan required for food production/repacking
📦 UK GPSR 2024: every product must have a UK-based Responsible Person whose name + address is on product/packaging
🏷️ English language instructions and warnings required for UK market
📋 Country of origin must be accurately stated (not 'Imported')
🚫 No false or misleading product claims (Consumer Protection from Unfair Trading Regulations 2008)
⚠️ Product images must reflect actual product — no misleading visuals or stock images of different versions
📦 EAN or UPC required for Amazon listings (unless Brand Registered with GTIN exemption)
♻️ Producer Responsibility: register with packaging waste scheme if turnover/tonnage above thresholds
